name: Llama Guard (Meta LLM Safety)
pricing_notes: Open source (Llama 3 Community License); 1B/8B parameter models on HuggingFace

auth.notes: Local inference via HuggingFace; HF token for gated model download

performance.notes: 1B model: fast on CPU (~200ms); 8B model: GPU recommended; classification task, not generation

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's LLM-based safety classifier for input/output moderation. Detects: violent speech, sexual content, hate speech, criminal planning, weapons, drugs, CBRN, and more. 11+ safety categories. Works on both prompts (input) and completions (output). Can be used inline in agent pipelines for content moderation. Self-hostable. Used in RLHF preference labeling. 1B variant suitable for CPU inference. Alternative to OpenAI Moderation API for self-hosted LLM deployments.

gotchas: False positive rate can impact user experience
Jailbreak resistance not complete — determined adversarial users can bypass
Llama 3 Community License (commercial use allowed with conditions)
Not a complete safety solution — defense in depth still needed
best_when: Moderation pipeline for self-hosted LLM deployments
Agent pipelines needing input/output safety classification
Alternative to OpenAI Moderation API without vendor dependency
avoid_when: Production systems needing guaranteed adversarial robustness
summary: Llama Guard is Meta's LLM-based safety classifier for detecting harmful content in LLM inputs and outputs. 11+ safety categories (violence, sexual content, hate speech, CBRN, etc.). Self-hostable 1B/8B models. Alternative to OpenAI Moderation API for self-hosted deployments. Used in agent pipelines for input/output moderation. Gated HuggingFace model requiring approval.
